exemplar.title: Investigating the effect of a cart mass of the acceleration of a cart in the presence of friction.

detailedExemplarData.title: Investigating the effect of a cart mass of the acceleration of a cart in the presence of friction.

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data tables are comprehensive, showing units, averages and propagated uncertainties
Graphs include titles, axes labels, error bars and clear presentation of processing steps
Processing of raw data into accelerations is accurate and clearly documented
Use of multiple line fits to estimate gradient uncertainty demonstrates sophisticated error analysis
weaknesses: Uncertainties for mass and gradient are not consistently propagated through every calculation
Rounding inconsistencies appear in some derived quantities
No statistical treatment beyond simple averages (e.g., standard deviation)
Justification for selection of steepest and shallowest fits is not shown
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The data analysis is both clear and precise: measurement recordings, propagation of uncertainties, and graphical presentations are of a consistently high standard. Although there are minor omissions in propagation for certain quantities and rounding decisions, these do not detract from the overall rigor and transparency. The work aligns with the highest band descriptor for data analysis.

strengths: Research question embedded in a specific motorsport context enhancing relevance
Detailed apparatus list with uncertainties and clear schematic enabling reproducibility
Step-by-step procedure logically ordered with sufficient detail for another investigator
Explicit control of variables and justification in most cases demonstrating methodological rigor
weaknesses: Choice of five trials per mass value is not justified statistically
No calibration checks recorded for measuring tape and stopwatch
String length and pulley friction details are omitted
Coefficient of static versus dynamic friction is mislabelled in preliminary discussion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student situates the research question in a concrete motorsport setting, provides a comprehensive materials list (with uncertainties), and offers a reproducible procedure with only minor gaps. Methodological considerations are explained at a high level. While a few specific details (trial justification, calibration steps, friction labelling) are missing, the overall design meets the descriptor for the top band. Thus a full score of 6 is merited.

strengths: Conclusion correctly restates the observed inverse relationship and cites specific data points
Consistency between the stated conclusion and the processed results is maintained
Links back to Newtonâs second law and friction theory, demonstrating conceptual awareness
weaknesses: Numerical value of the coefficient of friction and its uncertainty are omitted
No quantitative comparison to accepted literature values
Discussion remains largely qualitative without integration of error ranges
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is consistent with the analysis and brings in relevant theoretical context, fulfilling the criteria for band 3â4. However, it stops short of incorporating the quantitative outcome (Î¼_d and its uncertainty) and does not situate the result against accepted values. A band-4 score of 4 accurately reflects this level of performance.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses (e.g., track tilt, balance fluctuations, ruler precision) are identified
Realistic improvements (use of water level, digital metre, improved alignment jig) are described
Error sources are categorised as random and systematic in a clear table
weaknesses: Relative impact of each error source is not explained or quantified
No ranking or estimates of how suggested improvements would reduce bias/uncertainty
Explanation of improvement effects remains descriptive rather than analytical
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation identifies several concrete limitations and proposes relevant improvements, matching the descriptor for band 3â4. While the impact of each weakness and the quantitative benefit of the suggestions are not explained, the level of analysis is consistent with a mid-band score of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student formulates a clear research question (effect of cart mass on acceleration in the presence of friction) and embeds it within a specific context: a trackâpulley system motivated by observations from motorsport.  The background discussion links Newtonâs second law, friction and racing examples, demonstrating an appropriate, concrete setting rather than a generic statement.  Minor language issues do not detract from the overall specificity, so a high-band placement is warranted.

Instrument uncertainties are stated and propagated for time, displacement and acceleration; error bars appear on graphs.  Yet uncertainties for mass and gradient are treated somewhat inconsistently and not propagated through every calculation, leaving notable omissions.  This aligns with the mid-band descriptor.

Relevant methodological considerations (control of track type, temperature, hanging mass, distance; repetition; instrument precision) are presented and briefly justified.  However, the rationale for choosing only five cart-mass values and the adequacy of displacement measurement are not fully explained, so the strand does not reach the âexplainedâ level.

The student identifies several specific weaknesses (track tilt, balance fluctuations, ruler precision) but offers little explanation of their relative impactâmany are dismissed as ânegligibleâ without evidence.  This matches the descriptor for describing weaknesses rather than fully explaining impact.

Apparatus lists, photographs, a schematic and a numbered procedure are supplied.  Distances, masses, timer precision and number of trials are stated, enabling another investigator to reconstruct the setup with only minimal interpretation (e.g., exact string length is not critical).  Overall detail meets the descriptor of a method that can be reproduced.

Acceleration is calculated appropriately from kinematics and a gradient method is used to obtain Î¼_d, but numerical accuracy is uneven (e.g., rounding, sign of gradient) and no statistical treatment is applied beyond simple averages.  These issues constitute significant, though not crippling, inaccuracies.

Realistic improvements are suggestedâusing a water level, digital meter and clearer tapeâbut the student stops at description and does not elaborate on how each change would quantitatively improve reliability or validity.  Hence the strand fits the mid-band description level.

Data tables include units, uncertainties, averages and propagated errors; graphs carry titles, axes and error bars.  Processing steps are shown.  Presentation is generally neat and unambiguous, satisfying the requirement for both clarity and precision, despite a few typographical slips.

Links to Newtonâs second law and friction theory give some comparison with accepted scientific context, but the discussion is brief and lacks depth (no quantitative comparison to published Î¼ values or limiting-case analysis).  Thus the comparison is relevant but only partially developed.

The conclusion restates the inverse massâacceleration relationship and cites specific data points, maintaining consistency with the processed results.  However, it remains largely qualitative and does not integrate the numerical value of Î¼_d or discuss uncertainty ranges, so it falls just short of the top band.

annotations.comment: The student effectively grounds the research question within a real-world motorsport context, linking Newtonâs second law and friction to racing examples, which enhances the specificity and relevance of the investigation.
Some introductory sentences contain typographical and grammatical inconsistencies that may distract the reader; a focused proofreading pass will clarify the expression of key concepts.
The research question is embedded in the title but would benefit from being explicitly phrased as a question in the introduction to improve clarity and reader engagement.
The schematic diagram (Figure 1) is well labelled and provides a clear visual representation of the experimental setup, aiding reproducibility.

The description of static friction mislabels the coefficient of dynamic friction <math>\mu_d</math> as the static coefficient. Distinguish <math>\mu_s</math> and <math>\mu_d</math> clearly and correct the subscript.
The net force equation would benefit from vector notation to distinguish applied force and frictional force, improving precision in theoretical development.
Including displacement as an independent variable is incorrect; displacement is derived from time and acceleration and should be listed as a dependent or controlled parameter.
The hypothesis is clearly stated, linking increased cart mass with reduced acceleration under frictional resistance, demonstrating conceptual understanding.
The controlled variables table is incomplete with missing entries; ensure each control, its method of control and justification are fully documented to avoid ambiguity.
The equipment list includes precise uncertainties for each instrument, which significantly enhances reproducibility and transparency in measurement.
The preliminary work section lists five repetitions but does not justify this choice in terms of statistical uncertainty; include a rationale based on desired confidence or error reduction.
Include a calibration step for the measuring tape and stopwatch (e.g., use a standard length and a known time source) to substantiate claimed precision.
The step-by-step procedure is detailed and logically ordered, allowing reproduction of the experiment with minimal interpretation.
There is no mention of the string length or any measurement of pulley friction; add these details to ensure a fully reproducible setup.
Specify how the start-point tape alignment was verified between trials (e.g., use of a fixed jig) to avoid systematic variations in initial position.
The data table is comprehensive, including measured times, averages, uncertainties and derived accelerations, which demonstrates precise recording and processing.
Uncertainty in time is calculated but not consistently propagated to acceleration in each trial; extend error propagation through all calculations.
Add the mass measurement uncertainties to the main data table so that all sources of error are visible together and no uncertainty is overlooked.
The derivation of acceleration using kinematics and substitution of numerical values is executed correctly, demonstrating solid analytical skills.
The acceleration vs mass graph caption omits mention of error bars and axis units; ensure both are described for full precision.
Include the equation of the best-fit line and its RÂ² value on the acceleration vs mass graph to quantify the strength of the inverse relationship.
The table of resultant force vs system mass is well structured and enables clear subsequent analysis of frictional effects.
The method for selecting steepest and shallowest lines to estimate gradient uncertainty is not justified; detail the criteria or algorithm used.
Discuss any non-linearity or curvature in the plot of resultant force vs system mass to evaluate the validity range of the linear friction model.
Using multiple line fits to estimate the gradient and its uncertainty is commendable, demonstrating careful consideration of systematic variation.
The calculation of <math>\mu_d</math> omits any uncertainty contribution from <math>g</math>; indicate whether its variation is negligible or include propagation.
Compare the experimentally determined coefficient of friction (<math>\mu_d = 0.02 \pm 0.008</math>) with published steel-on-steel values to situate results in scientific context.
The conclusion accurately restates the qualitative inverse relation between mass and acceleration but omits the numerical <math>\mu_d</math> value and its uncertainty.
Integrate the determined coefficient of friction and its uncertainty into the conclusion to reinforce the quantitative outcome of the investigation.
The evaluation table systematically lists random and systematic errors, demonstrating an understanding of multiple uncertainty sources.
While multiple error sources are identified, the relative impact of each (e.g., tilt vs stopwatch precision) is not explained; rank and quantify where possible.
Propose a quantitative estimate of how using a water level to correct track tilt would reduce bias, improving validity by a calculable margin.

rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
rubricData.name: Physics IA
